license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.4tjgwkn
title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol 13: Trizol/RNAqueous Midi-Kit

steps_raw.instruction: Quickly weigh out 3 g of frozen tissue and place in a pre-chilled mortar containing liquid nitrogen.
Grind the sample into fine powder without allowing it to thaw.
Add the sample into a 50 ml centrifuge tube containing 30 mL of extraction buffer (10 ml buffer/g tissue).
Vortex for 00:01:00 , then incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Add 6 mL of chloroform (2 ml chloroform/g tissue) and shake vigorously for at least 00:00:20 .
Centrifuge at for 00:20:00 at 4 °C .
Move supernatant to new 50 ml tube. Add one volume isopropanol (about 22.5 mL ) to precipitate RNA.
Invert gently several times. Then incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Wash pellet with 45 mL of 70 % ethanol. (1/2)
Air dry pellet for 00:05:00 (if not completely dry, it’s still okay to move on to the next step).
The following steps use the RNAqueous Midi Kit from Ambion (AM1911) . Dissolve the pellet in 5 mL of lysis/binding Solution. Heating at 37 °C and vortexing will help dissolve pellet.
Heat 4.5 mL of elution solution to 100 °C (for later use). Use a 17 X 100 mm round bottom sterile polypropylene tube with loose-fitting dual position cap.
Add 5 mL of 64 % ethanol to RNA in lysis/binding solution.

Remove needle and attach filter unit. Slowly push the lysate/ethanol mixture through. Often times the filter gets clogged. There are tips in the kit manual for dealing with this but we found these did not help. We tried to get the solution through one filter even with some intense pressure. Sometimes two filters were required and the following wash/elution steps were performed on both filters.
After filtering the solution, force air through using a clean 10 ml syringe until no more white foam is expelled (at least 3 or 4 times).
Wash with 100 % volume of Wash Solution #1 (using syringe). Use a clean 18 g needle to draw up solution.
Force air through a few times again.
Wash with 70 % volume Wash Solution #2/3. Repeat once using syringe.
Force air through again until no more water droplets or fine spray can be seen.
Elute at least 2 times into a 2 ml tube (only use 500 µL at a time, so elute three times per tube to get about 1.5 ml total) using 100 °C Elution Solution and a sterile 3 ml syringe.
LiCl precipitate each sample using the LiCl provided in the kit.
DNase Treatment using the TURBO DNA-free kit from ambion (AM1907)
LiCl precipitate the supernatant again (same as above). The final resuspension volume should be between 50 µL – 100 µL using RNase free H 2 O.
Invert gently several times.
Draw into a 10 ml syringe through an 18 gauge needle.
Add ½ volume of LiCl.
Place at -20 °C for at least 00:30:00 .
Centrifuge at maximum speed in a microcentrifuge (about ) for 00:15:00 at Room temperature .
Wash pellet with 1 mL of 70 % ethanol.
Centrifuge again with the same conditons for 00:05:00 .
Remove supernatant and air dry pellet.
Resuspend in 50 µL or 100 µL RNase free H 2 O. Heating and vortexing can help with this.
Add 1.5 µL of DNase + 0.1 volumes of 10X buffer.
Incubate at 37 °C for 00:25:00 .
Add another 1.5 µL of DNase.
Incubate at 37 °C for another 00:25:00 .
Add 0.1 volumes DNase Inactivation Reagent.
Incubate 00:05:00 at Room temperature , flicking tubes every minute.
Centrifuge at for 00:01:30 .
Move supernatant to a new tube.
Then incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge at for 00:02:00 at 4 °C . (1/2)
Wash pellet with 45 mL of 70 % ethanol. (2/2)
Centrifuge at for 00:02:00 at 4 °C . (2/2)
